questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Pattadakal temple complex located?
questions.explanation: Pattadakal lies in the Bagalkot district of Karnataka, in the Deccan region.

questions.q: Which dynasty built the temples at Pattadakal?
questions.explanation: The temples were built by the early (Badami) Chalukyas, mainly in the 7th and 8th centuries.

questions.q: What international designation does Pattadakal hold?
questions.explanation: Pattadakal was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1987.

questions.q: Pattadakal is famous as a meeting point of which two broad temple-architecture traditions of India?
questions.explanation: Pattadakal blends the northern Nagara and southern Dravida temple styles in a single complex.

questions.q: The temples of Pattadakal stand along the banks of which river?
questions.explanation: The complex is set on the banks of the Malaprabha River, a tributary of the Krishna.

questions.q: Most of the principal deities worshipped in the main temples of Pattadakal are dedicated to which Hindu god?
questions.explanation: The major temples at Pattadakal are predominantly dedicated to Shiva.

questions.q: What was the historical ceremonial role of Pattadakal for the Chalukya rulers?
questions.explanation: Pattadakal was a royal coronation centre, reflected in its old name Pattada-Kisuvolal meaning 'town of the coronation stone'.

questions.q: Pattadakal lies close to which other famous early Chalukyan heritage town?
questions.explanation: Pattadakal forms part of the early Chalukyan triangle with nearby Badami and Aihole.

questions.q: Which type of construction best describes most of the Pattadakal temples?
questions.explanation: Unlike the rock-cut caves of Badami, Pattadakal's monuments are structural, free-standing stone temples.

questions.q: Which is the largest and most celebrated temple at Pattadakal?
questions.explanation: The Virupaksha Temple is the largest and grandest of the Pattadakal monuments.

questions.q: The Virupaksha Temple at Pattadakal was built to commemorate a Chalukya victory over which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Queen Lokamahadevi had the Virupaksha Temple built to mark the Chalukya victory over the Pallavas of Kanchipuram.

questions.q: Which Chalukya queen is credited with commissioning the Virupaksha Temple?
questions.explanation: Queen Lokamahadevi, a queen of Vikramaditya II, commissioned the Virupaksha Temple around 740 CE.

questions.q: During whose reign was the great Virupaksha Temple constructed?
questions.explanation: The Virupaksha Temple was built during the reign of the Chalukya king Vikramaditya II in the mid-8th century.

questions.q: Which southern temple is said to have inspired the design of the Virupaksha Temple at Pattadakal?
questions.explanation: The Virupaksha Temple was modelled on the Kailasanathar Temple of Kanchipuram, encountered after the Chalukya conquest of the Pallavas.

questions.q: What is the predominant building material of the Pattadakal temples?
questions.explanation: The temples were carved largely from the local reddish-gold sandstone of the Malaprabha valley.

questions.q: Besides Hindu temples, Pattadakal also contains a shrine of which other religion on its outskirts?
questions.explanation: A Jain temple, built later under the Rashtrakutas, stands at Pattadakal alongside the Hindu temples.

questions.q: Roughly how many major temples make up the core monument group at Pattadakal?
questions.explanation: The protected core group comprises about ten principal temples plus several smaller shrines.

questions.q: In which year was Pattadakal inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List?
questions.explanation: Pattadakal was added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1987 for its eclectic temple architecture.

questions.q: Which temple at Pattadakal is a near-twin of the Virupaksha Temple, built by another queen?
questions.explanation: The Mallikarjuna Temple, commissioned by Queen Trailokyamahadevi, closely resembles the adjacent Virupaksha Temple.

questions.q: Which is among the earliest surviving temples at Pattadakal, built in the Dravida style under King Vijayaditya?
questions.explanation: The Sangameshwara Temple, built under Vijayaditya in the early 8th century, is one of the oldest Dravida-style temples at the site.

questions.q: The narrative reliefs on Pattadakal's temples famously depict scenes from which Hindu epics?
questions.explanation: Sculpted panels at Pattadakal illustrate episodes from the Ramayana and Mahabharata, along with Puranic tales.

questions.q: Which architectural feature, a hallmark of southern temples, crowns the Virupaksha Temple?
questions.explanation: The Virupaksha Temple is topped by a tiered, pyramidal Dravida-style vimana over its sanctum.

questions.q: Which Pattadakal temple shows a marked northern Nagara style with a curvilinear tower?
questions.explanation: The Galaganatha Temple is a clear example of the curvilinear Nagara (rekha-nagara) shikhara at Pattadakal.

questions.q: The Virupaksha Temple's inscriptions name a chief architect honoured with which title?
questions.explanation: An inscription records the master architect Gunda, honoured with the title Tribhuvanacharya, the maker of the southern temple.

questions.q: Facing the Virupaksha Temple is a separate pavilion housing which sculpted figure?
questions.explanation: A detached Nandi mandapa in front of the Virupaksha Temple shelters a large stone image of Shiva's bull Nandi.

questions.q: The Papanatha Temple at Pattadakal is notable for blending which two styles within one structure?
questions.explanation: The Papanatha Temple uniquely combines Nagara and Dravida features, illustrating the experimental fusion at Pattadakal.

questions.q: The Jain temple at Pattadakal was added under which dynasty that succeeded the early Chalukyas?
questions.explanation: The Jain Narayana temple at Pattadakal was built in the 9th century during Rashtrakuta rule.

questions.q: What is the older Kannada name of Pattadakal, referring to its coronation stone?
questions.explanation: Pattadakal was historically known as Kisuvolal or Pattada-Kisuvolal, the 'red town of the coronation stone'.

questions.q: The early Chalukya capital from which the Pattadakal temples drew patronage was located at which town?
questions.explanation: Badami, ancient Vatapi, was the capital of the early Chalukyas who patronised the Pattadakal temples.

questions.q: Pattadakal, Badami and Aihole together are often described as a region that earned what reputation in Indian architecture?
options: A cradle of Hindu temple architecture
questions.explanation: The Aihole-Badami-Pattadakal cluster is celebrated as a cradle where Indian temple architecture was experimented with and matured.
